summary_one_line: Input must declare and use an ID-typed attribute so parsing populates cur->ids, making the free-time table non-NULL.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A valid XML doc whose parse registers at least one ID attribute (a DTD declaring an attribute of type ID plus an element carrying that attribute, e.g. an `id="..."` / `xml:id="..."` token), so cur->ids becomes non-NULL and tree.c:1228 takes its true branch at free time.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winner seeds carry the structural scaffolding to reach ID registration: `<?xml version="1.0"` prolog, `<!DOCTYPE` with `SYSTEM`, and an explicit ID attribute token `l:id=" :id="` (Seed 3); the winner takes the blocker true side on every trial (L1228 W=T18 F0) and exercises name/attribute parsing far more heavily (xmlParseName W=117 vs L=28, xmlParseStartTag2 W=24 vs L=72 with different paths).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser seeds reach xmlFreeDoc but never register an ID, so cur->ids stays NULL and L1228 is always false (L1228 L=T0 F20); loser inputs are malformed/garbage fragments (e.g. `#IMP`, `^^^^^`, broken URLs) that fall into element-creation paths the winner skips (xmlNewNode/xmlNewDocNode/xmlBuildQName/xmlParseNmtoken hit only in L) rather than completing ID-attribute parsing.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog adds I2SRandReplace over value_profile's identical CMP_MAP feedback; I2S splices logged comparison operands (the DTD/attribute keyword bytes and the ID-type literals the parser compares against) directly into the input at their byte pre-image, letting vpc assemble the exact `version=`, `DOCTYPE`, `SYSTEM`, and `id` token sequence that drives an ID registration, which value_profile's Hamming-distance gradient never closes to an exact match.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the true side of an ID-table presence test in xmlFreeDoc, reached only when cur->ids is non-NULL.
The winner takes the blocker true side on every trial while the loser never does.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L1228  T=18 F=0  T=0 F=20  if (cur->ids != NULL) xmlFreeIDTable((xmlIDTablePtr) cur-...

evidence_trail.claim: A winning seed contains an explicit id-attribute token sequence.
Winning seeds carry well-formed XML prolog and DOCTYPE scaffolding needed before ID registration.
Loser seeds fall into element-creation paths (xmlNewNode/xmlBuildQName) that the winner never executes, indicating they never complete ID-attribute parsing.
The winner exercises name parsing far more than the loser, consistent with completing a full named/attributed document.
value_profile_cmplog's only added technique over value_profile is the I2SRandReplace mutator.
value_profile lacks I2SRandReplace and relies on CMP_MAP distance buckets only.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 6929 --role W --fuzzer value_profile_cmplog --trial 1 --seed c20581314eb72916` and observed 1 I2S-floor dash row (mutation_op = -) at depth 6 of the 7-level ancestor chain, the ParentInfo-only signature of an unwrapped I2SRandReplace find under the current build.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness where value_profile resolves the cur->ids-non-NULL branch at the same rate as value_profile_cmplog despite requiring an exact ID-attribute keyword match, showing CMP_MAP gradient alone suffices and I2S substitution is not the enabling factor.

weakest_evidence_point: The id-attribute token appears in only one winning seed (31a0f4cf800d3991); the other winners show DTD/SYSTEM scaffolding but not an explicit id token at a fixed offset, so the precise byte requirement for ID registration is inferred from source/hit-count rather than uniformly present across all five winners.
